name: Score
goal: Display a color-coded numeric score pill that auto-tiers itself into high, med, or low based on the value-to-max ratio.
description: Small inline pill showing a numeric score with semantic color coding: green for high, amber for med, red for low. Tier auto-computes from value/max (default max=10 matches the dominant 0–10 corpus scale). Explicit tier attribute overrides auto-computation. Global thresholds are overridable via --ce-score-breakpoints. Exposes role=meter ARIA semantics.

props.name: value
props.description: The numeric score. Drives auto-tier computation and ARIA aria-valuenow.

props.name: max
props.description: Denominator for tier computation. Defaults to 10 because the corpus primarily uses 0–10 and 0–5 rating scales.

props.name: tier
props.description: Explicit tier override: "high" (green) | "med" (amber) | "low" (red). When empty, tier is auto-computed from value/max against the breakpoint thresholds. 3-value style enum per CDR-001.

props.name: inline
props.description: When true (default), the pill is inline-flex for use in table cells and inline text. Set to false for a block-level standalone score.

slots.description: Label override. When empty, the formatted value (1 decimal, e.g. 9.4) is shown. Supply custom content such as 9.4★ for rich decoration.

cssVariables.name: --ce-score-breakpoints
cssVariables.description: Two space-separated ratio thresholds for auto-tier: '<high-threshold> <med-threshold>'. Scores >= high-threshold → high, >= med-threshold → med, else → low. Override at :root to change the global threshold policy (CDR-003). Example: "0.8 0.5".

cssVariables.name: --ce-color-green
cssVariables.description: Text and border color for tier=high.

cssVariables.name: --ce-color-amber
cssVariables.description: Text and border color for tier=med.

cssVariables.name: --ce-color-red
cssVariables.description: Text and border color for tier=low.

a11y.notes: The host element carries role="meter", aria-valuenow (the score), aria-valuemin="0", aria-valuemax (the max), and aria-valuetext ("<value> of <max>"). These are set in willUpdate so they stay in sync with value/max changes.
